entry.title: Inaugural SAP Stammtisch Wollongong
entry.content:
How often do you get to go to the first edition of a classic event?

The inaugural SAP Stammtisch Wollongong will take place on Saturday March 15th. It will be at Pepe's On The Beach, which is very close to North Wollongong station.  We will be there from 1.30pm so just come along when you can.

Please spread the word to anyone you can think of, so that we can make it a great catch up of old mates. New faces are most welcome too of course. Reach out to me if you have any questions

This page (on Sydney Stammtisch) provides some background info on what to expect

#SAPStammtischGong

entry.title: Live profile view Variable Pay-Enhancement
entry.content:
Hi

Looking to understand if it is possible and how to enhance the Live Profile View functionality in Variable Pay to including any Custom fields ( such as Absence Factor affecting Final Bonus) that may be relevant to show to the Users for calculation of Final Bonus? 

Does this require incorporating these custom fields directly into the XML ?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for group reporting - Release 2502 is generally available
entry.content: Thanks for the detailed overview, Stéphane! The new features look fantastic.

entry.title: New Beginnings: Introducing the Beyond SAP Cloud channel on YouTube
entry.content:
Check out our "Beyond SAP Cloud - YouTube" channel.

Wouter Lemaire and I attended SAP Insider Copenhagen 2024, where we had the chance to meet SAP Champion Abdullah Galal 🏅 . He introduced us to his excellent YouTube channel, which primarily covers SAP Finance topics. If you haven’t seen it yet, you can check it out here: @AbdullahGalal. His content is insightful and absolutely worth watching!

After our conversation, he inspired us to explore YouTube as a way to share our own knowledge. We realized it could help us reach a broader audience while staying true to what we love: sharing knowledge, learning, and growing together. His enthusiasm convinced us to take the leap—so here we are, launching our own channel!

Over the past few years, we’ve been conducting workshops with customers, focusing on best practices for governance and administration of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). We have a wealth of content to draw from, covering key topics on running SAP BTP efficiently from a customer perspective. This will be our initial focus for the channel, but we’re already brainstorming additional ideas. We also plan to invite SAP Mentors and other experts to join us in sharing valuable insights with the SAP community through our YouTube channel Beyond SAP Cloud.

Wouter and I have already had a blast recording the first videos, and we’re excited to gradually release them over time. The first batch will include an introduction video along with several short clips to kick off this new knowledge-sharing journey.

Stay tuned! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Blending Live data with import data option in Model o...
entry.content:
Hello,

This is because blending in Tables is not yet implemented in the Optimized stack.

For the moment you can link dimensions and then use the linked models in blended visualizations, but not yet in tables.

Blending in tables (whatever the connection type) is currently planned for QRC3 2025.

Regards

Laura
SAP BDC&I P&A Product Manager

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: Thanks for the detailed explanation @RavdeepSingh

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Mobile Services Native/MDK App Authorization...
entry.content: Hi @bill_froelich, thank you for the information! I do not get the idea of the Security tab of the MDK app then. It has several security options, e.g., API Key, Basic, SAML, HTTP, etc. It has XSUAA settings, role settings, provides also option for the anonymous access - why all these options are there and how can these be used if is OAuth2 only and IdP needs to be configured via BTP?

entry.title: Re: Set the value for start date of THISWEEK semantic ope...
entry.content:
Fulfilled the requirement by writing the below code snippet within the onInit method of the controllers extension:

sap.ui.core.LocaleData.getInstance(sap.ui.getCore().getConfiguration().getFormatSettings().getFormatLocale()).mData["weekData-firstDay"] = 1;

entry.title: Re: Unable to Post Payload to SOAP API using SAP CPI !
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you try using the Message Mapping element with the WSDL file available for the API?

Usually with this it already considers the Content-Type settings and correct namespace. Passing the payload manually and also adding the headers through a content modifier then should be considered the Content-Type = "text/xml".

https://api.sap.com/api/WORKFORCEPERSONMASTERDATAREPLI/overview

Looking forward for a positive outcome. 

Thank you 

Tiago Ribeiro

But while uploading the data from an excel im facing error as -@5C\QError@ S:RPM_UTILITIES:155 PC23269 1 and upload is failing. are you aware of this error? my system doesn't have message 155 in rpm_utilities. 

can you please help

entry.title: Local Roots, Global Impact: How Gruponabeiro Navigated the Business Landscape in Angola
entry.content:
As we continue our journey through a dynamic and ever-changing global landscape, we're thrilled to bring you the next captivating story in our ongoing blog series, featuring remarkable tales of innovation and resilience. 
In this exciting installment, we spotlight the incredible journey of Gruponabeiro, a Portugal-based coffee powerhouse, and their impressive strides in the complex market of Angola. 

Gruponabeiro: A Journey of Aromatic Transformation and Resilient Growth

In the aromatic and culturally rich world of coffee, stories of growth, triumph, and innovation abound. Among these, the journey of Portugal-based Gruponabeiro stands out as a remarkable tale of perseverance and transformation. Celebrating over 61 years of operation, the company, founded by Manuel Rui Azinhais Nabeiro, affectionately known as Mr. Rui Nabeiro, has woven an inspiring narrative of overcoming challenges in one of the world's most complex yet rewarding markets – Angola.

Gruponabeiro’s journey, which began in 1961 with a small coffee startup in Campo Maior, Portugal, has now expanded its footprints across eight countries. This transformation from a local coffee roaster to an international powerhouse is nothing short of extraordinary. Mr. Nabeiro’s vision was not simply about business but about touching lives and empowering communities through the universal language of coffee.

This commitment to transformation and resilience resonated profoundly with Mr. Jose Beato, the Managing Director of Africa and Latin America for Gruponabeiro. During our conversation, Mr. Beato shared insightful stories about Gruponabeiro's remarkable transformation journey, particularly their significant ventures in Angola and managing local regulatory compliance.

Navigating Business Complexities Through Localization

One of Mr. Nabeiro's most significant business moves was the company's successful entry into Angola. Unlike any previous logistical endeavors, entering the Angolan market was no simple feat. The company had to adapt to a less developed business environment while complying with local regulations.

Successfully entering the Angolan market required Gruponabeiro to overcome various operational challenges, particularly in meeting local tax and regulatory requirements. Under Mr. Nabeiro's leadership, the company embarked on a mission to address these challenges without hindering business operations.

In 2019, the implementation of the Value-Added Tax (VAT) system in Angola marked a significant shift in the local economy. Recognizing the need for robust digital solutions, Gruponabeiro adopted SAP to streamline its operations and ensure compliance with the new tax regulations. This technological integration proved beneficial throughout their growth journey, allowing them to manage their business processes efficiently even in a complex environment.

An important achievement and testament to their preparedness and quick adaptation was successfully implementing SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax) compliance, a measure adopted by the Angolan authorities. By leveraging capabilities of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Gruponabeiro not only championed this critical transition smoothly but also led the process with local authorities, ensuring that they remained at the forefront of digital compliance.

The implementation of SAP facilitated the company's efficient handling of regulatory requirements and operational challenges, allowing it to focus on its mission of growth and sustainability in Angola. The benefits of localization through SAP enabled Gruponabeiro to remain agile and responsive in a complex market, ensuring their long-term success and commitment to the local economy.

Sustainable Business Through Localized Efforts

Gruponabeiro’s presence in Angola is grounded in environmental and social sustainability. The company began buying coffee from local farmers, offering fair prices and restoring an industry devastated by a 40-year war.

"We decided to buy coffee from local farmers, offering fair prices, and to reestablish the virtually extinct coffee industry," notes Mr. Beato. "We began purchasing coffee from local producers step by step, visiting areas that had not seen buyers in decades."

By 2000, Gruponabeiro started selling Delta coffee in Angola, and by 2001, they began industrial roasting operations of Ginga coffee. Ginga, named after an ancient queen renowned for her resistance against European colonization, had deep cultural significance and symbolized resilience to the Angolan people.

Mr. Beato’s decade-long experience in Angola was transformative. He forged profound connections with local culture and people through the coffee revival efforts. "My life experience there was profound," recounts Mr. Beato. " I will be a little bit always African for my life."

Gruponabeiro's localized approach has brought sustainable change, aiding local farmers and revitalizing small-scale coffee farming in remote areas lacking infrastructure.

Summary

Gruponabeiro’s journey from a humble coffee venture in Portugal to an international powerhouse underscores the importance of localization. By leveraging SAP’s localized capabilities, Gruponabeiro successfully navigated Angola’s complex regulatory environment and ensured sustainable business growth. Their commitment to supporting local farmers and adhering to local regulations highlights the power of a strong entrepreneurial spirit and a deep understanding of local markets.

As Gruponabeiro continues to transform its business and explore new territories, their journey in Angola serves as a testament to the values and benefits of localization, demonstrating the importance of adapting to and understanding local markets while fostering sustainable growth.

Learn about Localization Today 

Angola - Enterprise Resource Planning Localization Work Zone

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community

SAP Localization Community 

Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease

entry.title: SAP IBP Adjusted Transport Supply is not getting fulfilled even if there is enough initial inventory
entry.content:
In Supply Optimizer, IBP doesn't fulfill the Adjusted Transport Supply requirement even if there is enough initial inventory. IBP is retaining some inventory to be supplied to future weeks.

The cost model assigned was no safety stock violation and there is a high inventory holding cost. 

The requirement of business is to push all available stock to fulfill the transport supply.

Thanks. J

entry.title: Re: Sap B1 Hana SP 2402 Registering B1WebServer into SLD...
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Did you able to resolve the issue? I am facing same issue.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic restricted key figure in analytic model
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi,

Normally we would create a Date Dimension that has flags or relative periods/years attributes, this can then be used as an association on the fact based on the appropriate date.

Then in the analytical model you can create a restricted measure that uses the dimension attributes to return the current year/period data.

Rich T

entry.title: Condition Contract - Automatic pricing conditions determination in CC
entry.content:
Dear Guru,

In an S/4HANA project, we have implemented Condition Contracts for Sales. We need to automatically determine pricing conditions when the condition contract is created or saved. We have been searching for a standard feature that allows this (such as a process variant or an event for the transfer manager), but we have not found any suitable option.

Could you please suggest what kind of parameterization we can follow or which BADI can be used?

Thank you,
Michele

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
This setting is only for the authentication between IAS and the Admin interface and I don't think you can change this as it is a system application.

Or to put it more clearly: There's not even a need to change the protocol for this application. The protocol is chosen separately for each application and each corporate IdP, and IAS "translates" between them as required.

entry.title: Re: Efficient ABAP Proxy Migration from SAP PO to Integration Suite

Thx for the blog. It is easy to work with.
One question though: you say that the object now reside in MDR and can no longer be changed from ESR.
But if do want to make changes to one of the objects. How do I do that? Like, which transactions do i use?

entry.title: Re: CAP Application getting 404 after deployment to CF
entry.content: Add "cds" : { "server": { "index": true } } to the package.json

entry.title: Re: I like, I wish
entry.content: This post asks for feedback on the course "Getting Started with Terraform on SAP BTP" that will start next week. Published a bit early ...

entry.title: Re: Will Deprecated Extensibility Objects (CDS, DATA Elements, etc.) be still usable after 2502 Rele
entry.content:
Hello,

CDS View Lifecycle Status process and Phases is described in the following HELP SAP Portal Document. 

Mainly, the decommissioning of one CDS View depends on their status and if Planned Decommission Date has already been defined once the CDS View has been changed to "Deprecated". 

Customers do have considerable timing to adopt the changes to new CDS Views replacing the previous ones. 

Here is an example of a deprecated CDS View. In the CDS View page, the Successor CDS Views is identified.  

In S4 Public Cloud "View Browser" Fiori App it's also possible to check deprecated CDS Views and in case they are used in Custom CDS View definition, such info is added into the impacted custom CDS Views which should then be acted upon by Administrators/Experts.

View Browser Fiori App

Custom CDS Views Fiori App with Task Priority Alert

 

Custom CDS View Fiori App with Task Category "Deprecation" identified

For ABAP Development it follows a similar framework. You can read more about this here. 

Hope this information helps understanding how to manage lifecycle of extensibility objects in your projects.

Thank you

Tiago Ribeiro

what could be the reason. can anyone guide me on this.

 #SAP PSM FM  # Fund Management.

entry.title: Can we upload attachments in custom Business Configuration Maintenance Objects(BCO) in SAP BTP?
Re: Fiori Elements ListReport with OData V4 - Cannot hide...
entry.content: One possible workaround could be to use setPV() method of the table building block based on your condition. See https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/3801656db27b4b7a9099b6ed5fa1d769 for more information.

entry.title: UNITS - workcenter (hour) / routing (min) ?
entry.content: Could be different unit in activity type between workcenter (hour) / routing (min) ?

entry.title: Re: How to export a excle file to local path?
entry.content: The steps for business users to upload CSV files are: 1. Log in to BO; 2. Go to the designated folder; 3. Right click ->new ->Local Document; 4. Select the file; 5. Click the add button to complete the CSV file upload.

entry.title: Re: Manage dates-driven planning processes with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Xiao,

Thank you for your message.

SAC has some standard Date calculation functions like "DateAdd", that could help to estimate a project end date from a start date and a number of days input:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/8b8f9ce9952a450ebd12b81cf5b608b7.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=release&q=dateadd

Nevertheless, the project end date calculation must usually consider a working days calendar to estimate a realistic end date. Thus I recommend you to calculate this working days calendar by month, then trigger the projects end date calculation through a data action instead of trying a direct calculation.

entry.title: What is API?
entry.content:
Introduction

APIs act as intermediaries, allowing two applications to communicate. This communication can involve sending and retrieving data or allowing different software components to interact and perform tasks.

 By defining a clear set of methods and tools, APIs ensure that different software can reliably interact regardless of their underlying architecture or technology. Because of this, APIs have played a critical role in the growth of the Internet, enabling cloud services, mobile application development, and the integration of web-based services. Today, APIs are much more than simple code libraries. They are sophisticated tools that define the way we interact with technology.

How do APIs work?

APIs work by exposing a limited number of actions and data points with which external software can interact. When a software system wants to access a resource provided by another system (such as data or functionality), it sends a request detailing the action it needs to perform. This request is made through an API. If the API is authorized, the system processes this request and sends back a response.

 APIs are often designed to be triggered by business events. An event is any action or change of state that is important to a business, such as when someone swipes a credit card, checks in for a flight, resets a password, or when inventory is updated in a warehouse. In this way, APIs are often used in event-driven Architecture to facilitate end-to-end processes where multiple systems are accessed to accomplish specific tasks related to the process. 

 Core components and structure

The core components and structure of an API include endpoints, methods, requests, and responses. Endpoints are specific addresses (URLs for web APIs) where APIs can be accessed. Methods are the allowable actions (such as GET, POST, PUT, DELETE for HTTP APIs) that can be performed at these endpoints. Requests include the required data and parameters to perform the action act, while responses are the data returned by the API.

 Today, APIs are primarily designed according to a schema that determines the rules of API interaction and how an API is formatted, validated, and documented. Structured to ensure secure, reliable, and efficient communication between systems, APIs include specifications for routines, data structures, object classes, and variables. 

Synchronous vs asynchronous APIs

Synchronous and asynchronous APIs refer to different approaches in the way software systems handle and respond to requests. These terms are commonly used in the context of programming and web development. 

 With synchronous APIs, when a request is made, the program blocks and waits for the operation to complete before moving on to the next task. This means that the application is "synchronized" with the operation, and it won't proceed until the requested action is finished. Synchronous APIs are often used when Simplicity and readability of code are crucial.

 With asynchronous APIs, when a request is made, the program continues to execute other tasks without waiting for the operation to finish. This often results in faster response times. Asynchronous APIs are often used when responsiveness and performance are critical.

What do APIs mean for businesses?

For businesses, APIs are more than just a technology; they are also a strategic asset that enables companies to leverage existing infrastructure and data, offering new ways to interact with customers, partners, and even competitors. APIs facilitate the creation of new business models, products, and services by enabling easy integration, secure data exchange, and rapid innovation.

 Through such new business models, companies can monetize their data and services and create new revenue streams. APIs, for example, are at the heart of the Software as a Service (SaaS) model, where businesses offer software applications over the Internet. Additionally, APIs have facilitated the rise of platform-based business models, where companies create a technological platform that other businesses can build upon, fostering a network of interdependent services and products.

entry.title: Re: Update Prod.Order Header (or) Operation Status by Function Module

entry.title: Re: New Beginnings: Introducing the Beyond SAP Cloud channel on YouTube
entry.content: Looking forward to watch & learn!

entry.title: Customer-specific data were not saved due to error CVI_MAPPING 042 (see long text!)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Can anyone tell us how we could resolve this issue? When we are trying to extend a new company code through FLCU00 in BP Role, the system is prohibiting the entry and showing the below message. 

 

 

 

Customer-specific data were not saved due to error CVI_MAPPING 042 (see long text!)

Message no. CVI_BDT_ADAPTER050

 

Diagnosis

While saving the customer-specific data of the business partner the following error occurred:

Customer VD44 already exists
CVI_MAPPING 042

The business partner was created/changed, the corresponding customer was not created/changed.

Background

In your system, the so-called post processing office (PPO) is active. The update of the customer-specific data is delayed (occurs technically "on commit"), while all non-customer-specific business partner data are updated directly after pressing the save button. Due to technical reasons a "normal" error message is not possible any more during "COMMIT WORK" processing. Because of this errors that occur during processing of customer-specific data are saved in PPO and the corresponding customer-specific database tables are not updated. This leads to the situation that pure customer-specific fields in transaction BP are empty after saving, although they had been filled with values before pressing the save button.

System Response

Certain customer-specific fields in transaction BP are empty after saving, although they had been filled with values before.

Procedure

Correct your entries according to the error message mentioned above (also available in the message short text in brackets) and save the business partner again. If needed contact your system administrator.

Procedure for System Administration

You can get further details related to the error directly in PPO. For accessing PPO start transaction MDS_PPO2 and enter the corresponding date on which the error occurred in field "Technical Creation Date". The concerned business partner should then be available in the result list.

Thank you
Hasnat

entry.title: CDS views of equipment types & groups in public cloud
entry.content: We are maintaining vehicle groups & vehicle types in the customizing (equipment types & equipment groups). I am not able to find the CDS view of equipment types & groups to get the data. Is there any way to get those values?

entry.title: Re: SAP RESTFUL ABAP custom table update along with RAP e...
entry.content:
@udayagiri_swamy 

The MODIFY table statement is not allowed during RAP interaction phase, including action handlers. Use unmanaged save and implement the MODIFY statement in save_modified method.

https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/fc4c71aa50014fd1b43721701471913d/2c2bec28fff6475f922f9f876491826d.html

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content: Hi Carl, Sadly, Expert Sessions are not recorded. Perhaps this webcast could provide you with a starting point. Additionally, check your inbox, as we send our webcast list and customer newsletter monthly so that you can find helpful information tailored to your needs.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Employee Central integration with third party Payroll
entry.content:
Hi Expect,

We recently integrated Employee Central, our core HR system, with a third-party payroll system, which is not SAP Employee Central payroll. We are facing massive problems, can you help indicate the appropriate Implementation guide for third-party payroll reference from SAP?

We are using the Replication of Employee Data from SAP ERP HCM to employee central using SAP cloud integration as Middleware.

Regards

Carolina

entry.title: Re: Extending a Standard API - A Guide to Enhancing API_QUALITYNOTIFICATION
entry.content: Is this approach considered clean core/Industry cloud compliant?

entry.title: Manage Prices Sales (F4111) - Access the data externally
entry.content:
The app does make pricing pretty easy, although accessing the data outside of the app seems impossible. Has anyone out there been able to fit all the pieces together?

We are currently implementing S/4HANA Public Edition at one of our customers, of which the 'Manage Prices' app is a part as well. That provides us functionality to set prices and import/export using Excel sheets.


Without access to the actual data tables (the classic Axxx tables), we have to use CDS views and public APIs to make our data available to any external parties. Which is fine, as long as those entry points exist. In this case however, they don't. The data list from the app itself would be great, but even that is unaccessible. You can understand we do no want any manual activity (like exporting an Excel sheet) whenever prices are updated.

In most documentation SAP mentions the API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV service, but that only provides the bare condition records, no link with the products or other data from the app. That does not help us here.

Internally for the app, SAP makes use of SD_PRICING_CONDITIONRECORD_SRV, which in turn calls on C_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDTP, a local Business Object Interface which retrieves the right data. Problem is, we're not allowed to use those ourselves. At least not to expose the data to the outside world.

There is a service for Sales Price Integration (SAP_COM_0702), but that provides prices per customer. We simply require all prices, as listed in the app, filtered by ConditionType, as an OData service.

So far I haven't been able to find any way to provide access to the data. Please tell me I'm missing something small and it's not one of those "please create a feature request" cases.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure "standard text key" for task list &...
entry.content: i did not get "Define Standard Text Key and create a new key." this option.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Transformation Center - Digital Blueprint
entry.content:
Hi @bjaganathan1 

you can connect your source SAP ECC system and target S/4HANA system as described in the documentation - I recommend to use the CIAS or do it manually following the docu: https://help.sap.com/docs/btc/setup-and-administration-guide/using-cloud-integration-automation-service-cias-for-setting-up-sap-business-transformation-center

It's embedded within SAP Cloud ALM and doesn't require additional license. 

Cheers

Matthias

entry.title: synchronous goods movement for Plant maintenance orders(Consumption)

entry.title: Re: Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of...

entry.title: Add Mass Excel Data Updates in any Standard SAP Fiori App Using the UI5 Spreadsheet Importer
entry.content:
Excel remains one of the most critical and widely used tools in the enterprise world, thanks to its versatility and flexibility. Therefore, whenever building new solutions, it is essential to integrate spreadsheet files into digital processes, especially if Excel cannot be fully eliminated.

To simplify this process, I have developed an open-source component that enables you to create entries from spreadsheet files, called UI5 Spreadsheet Importer, and more recently, also allows you to update multiple entries in bulk. The big advantage of this component is that it can be integrated into standard SAP applications. I have previously demonstrated how to do this with the “Manage Banks” application in an earlier blog post (How to add Excel Upload to any Standard SAP Fiori App (tutorial with App "Manage Banks").

In the meantime, many updates have been introduced both to the Spreadsheet Importer component and to how adaptation projects are handled with the Fiori tools. Currently, the UI5 Spreadsheet Importer supports the mass update feature only with OData V4. Therefore, in this blog post, we will use the new “Manage Banks” app, which uses an OData V4 service. Please note that creating new records works with OData V2 and the spreadsheet importer as well.

Deploy Spreadsheet Importer

To use the component, it must be deployed to the ABAP stack. This process has also improved since the last blog post and is documented here.

You can clone this GitHub repository and follow the instructions in the README.md. This repository contains a UI5 application that allows you to import all versions of the Spreadsheet Importer into your system. After deployment, you can reuse the Spreadsheet Importer component in any UI5 apps and adaptation projects.

Create Adaptation Project

The complete example described here can be found in a my GitHub repository.

The creation of adaptation projects has also been improved in the last two years. You can find an excellent tutorial here, and the official documentation is available here .

Here is how it can look like:

Add the Component

Adaptation projects can now also be created in VS Code. The following steps are shown in VS Code, but they do not differ much from SAP Business Application Studio. The general procedure is similar to the one in my previous blog post, although the UI might look a little different now.

First, right-click on the “webapp” folder and choose Add SAPUI5 Component Usages.

In the dialog, provide a Usage ID, for example "customer.upload", and set the component name to the version deployed on your system, for instance "cc.spreadsheetimporter.v1_7_3".

This process will create a file similar to id_1739737700495_addComponentUsages.change.

Adding Custom XML and Custom Controller

To add a button in the toolbar and an extension to trigger the Spreadsheet Importer with JavaScript, you can open the Adaptation Editor by right-clicking on “webapp” and selecting “Open Adaptation Editor”, or by running npm run start-editor from the terminal (as defined in your package.json).

Add Button to Action Toolbar

Hover over the table until it is highlighted with a blue frame, then use the editor’s button to add a new fragment to the Action Toolbar.

In the dialog, choose the “actions” aggregation with the fragment name "excelupload". This creates a new XML file named "excelupload.fragment.xml" under the “fragments” folder, which you can now edit as needed.

 This will create a change file and the XML Fragment.

 

Add Controller Extension

To open the Spreadsheet Importer, you need a Controller Extension. Again, hover over the table and click the button “Extend with Controller”. 

Provide a name for the extension file, for example "excelupload". A new file named "excelupload.js" is generated under the “coding” folder, containing boilerplate code for extending the List Report with JavaScript.

 Make sure to always save the changes. This is highlighted in the Adaption Editor:

This will create a change file and the javascript file.

Add JavaScript Method to Open Spreadsheet Importer

Next, add a method in the controller that will be invoked by our new button. This method calls the Spreadsheet Importer component using the usage ID "customer.upload" we defined earlier.

The Configuration Options are documented here, and these options allow you to tailor the component to your requirements.

In our example, we want to give users the ability to update data in mass. Therefore, we set the property action to "UPDATE". The parameter tableId indicates which table to use for updates. Usually, the best approach is to download your existing data first, so that key fields for identification are automatically included in the spreadsheet. Hence, we enable a download button by setting showDownloadButton to true.

We also configure deepDownloadConfig properties:

  • deepLevel: 0, because we only need the root level entity.
  • addKeysToExport: true, so that the spreadsheet will contain key fields for identification.
  • setDraftStatus: true, to handle drafts properly if the OData V4 service uses draft features.
  • showOptions: false, to skip any additional dialogs and immediately download the spreadsheet.

The final method looks like this:

 

async onUpload(event) {
    const spreadsheetUploadComponent = await this.base.getAppComponent().createComponent({
        usage: "customer.upload",
        async: true,
        componentData: {
            context: this,
            action: "UPDATE",
            tableId: "bf.bk.bank.manage::BankList--fe::table:‌‌:LineItem-innerTable",
            showDownloadButton: true,
            deepDownloadConfig: {
                deepLevel: 0,
                addKeysToExport: true,
                setDraftStatus: true,
                showOptions: false
            }
        }
    });

    spreadsheetUploadComponent.openSpreadsheetUploadDialog();
}

 

Add Button to Call Method

Finally, we add a button in the XML fragment to call our onUpload method. An example XML fragment could look like this:

 

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core='sap.ui.core' xmlns='sap.m'>
    <Button 
        id="excelUpload" 
        text="Excel Upload"
        press=".extension.customer.app.variant1.excelupload.onUpload"
    />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

Note that in the press event, the namespace of your extension must be included. For example, if your extension namespace is "customer.app.variant1" and the controller extension is named "excelupload", the method is "onUpload".

The Button now appears in the Toolbar:

 

Preview

You can now open the preview from the context menu (right-click on webapp and select “Preview Application”) or start it from the terminal with npm start. This will launch the application, and when you click on your new “Excel Upload” button, the Spreadsheet Importer dialog will appear.


From there, you can click “Download Data as Spreadsheet” to edit and then re-upload your data.

 In this example, you might add a new value (like a BIC code) for all banks in the spreadsheet and upload it.

The “Show Preview” button lets you see the data before finalizing the upload.

Clicking the “Upload” button then updates the data in the background.

Further Changes

A quick note of caution: Not every app can be adapted with the Adaptation Project, and each standard app may behave differently. Also, for the update feature, the underlying standard app must use an OData V4 service. Creating new records, however, is also possible with OData V2. Simply change the action to "CREATE" or add a separate button for creation.

Additionally, there are many other configuration options and events you can leverage to customize the component to your specific needs.

Deployment

For deployment of the adaptation project, you can follow this documentation .

Further Links

  • Landing Page UI5 Spreadsheet Importer
  • Live Demo Spreadsheet Importer and Mass Updates
  • Central Deployment of the Spreadsheet Importer
  • GitHub Repo for Packed Deployment to ABAP
  • Configuration Options for Spreadsheet Importer
  • SAPUI5 Adaptation Tutorial
  • Official Documentation for Adaptation Projects
  • Deployment Instructions for Adaptation Projects

entry.title: The Art and Science of CAP (part 9) with Daniel Hutzel
entry.content:
Join Daniel Hutzel, hosted by DJ Adams, live on their exploration of The Art and Science of CAP, sharing insights and ideas from the origins, philosophy and magic of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. #SAPCAP #TASC

See the series blog post The Art and Science of CAP for details, notes, links, other episodes and more.

entry.title: Re: How to find CDS Views - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and S/4HANA Private Cloud.
entry.content:
@freder1k_hanI recently created a CDS view in Eclipse ADT and reused it in the 'Custom CDS View' app, but I suppose it's not a good practice.

I just provided the correct annotations so that the 'Custom CDS View' app can 'see' the view from ADT.

entry.title: Local renaming of an IBP attribute within a MS Excel planning view
entry.content:
Dear community,

is it possible to overwrite the standard naming of an IBP attribute within an MS Excel planning view?
I tried to apply the renaming in an active IBP Excel planning view via Options > Member Names > Attributes but the selection box below attributes does not show any members at all. So I am wondering if it's possible to apply local names for attributes at all, e.g. instead of Product --> Material no.?

Example:

best regards, Peter

P.S. It works perfectly fine for attribute values and key figures. But actually there seems to be no option for attributes as a such.

entry.title: Re: Custom field appearing twice
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Custom Field Visibility and Enablement**:
- When you create custom fields, ensure that you have enabled their usage for all relevant user interfaces and APIs. It seems like there's a disconnect between the SAP GUI and the UI where the fields are displayed.

2. **SAP GUI Enablement**:
- Enabling the SAP GUI for the custom field could be causing the duplication on the UI if the field was initially intended for a different interface. Ensure the field is only enabled in the necessary contexts.

3. **Field Visibility in APIs**:
- When you noticed the field didn’t appear in the UI after creation via API, but it was in the database, it indicates that the field might not have been enabled correctly across all necessary UIs. Ensure the field is enabled for API usage in the "APIs" tab and check if it aligns with how custom fields should be used with API operations.

4. **Check Custom Field Definition**:
- It might be useful to revisit the Custom Fields app to ensure you haven’t configured the same custom field under two different contexts or mistakenly created two similar fields.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Groovy and learning basics
entry.content: Hi, Burak, I found this documentation, https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCT_ID/368c481cd6954bdfa5d0435479fd4eaf/script-use-cases?state=PRODUCTION&version=Cloud&locale=en-US, do you know other documentation different where can see all the methods that I can use with the object message?

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content: Seems like you´re looking more for a frontend solution? I think i would more look like backend solutions to provide the data faster in the backend.

Sorry, just for a clarity, no more Business Technology Platform (BTP) or Cloud Platform Integration(or CPI) or CI?.

Is Integration suite a component of BTP?.

entry.title: Re: How to attach FE OP Controller Extension function to...
entry.content:
I would suggest you attach to the List Binding Event:
https://ui5.sap.com/#/api/sap.ui.model.odata.v4.ODataListBinding%23events/dataReceived 

onAfterRendering: function () {
  var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();
  var oView = this.getView();
  var oTable = oView.byId("yourTableId"); // Replace with actual table ID
  
  if (oTable) {
    var oBinding = oTable.getBinding("items"); // Adapt this if you're using a different aggregation
    if (oBinding) {
      oBinding.attachEvent("dataReceived", function (oEvent) {
        console.log("Data received:", oEvent);
      });
    }
  }
}

entry.title: SAP SIGNAVIO PROCESS INTELLIGENCE: Unkown IP adress appearing during SAP RFC connection
entry.content:
We are trying to set up a connection between a SAP ECC 7.5 SP31 system and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Process Intelligence using the SAP RFC connection option ( no On premise connector). We are getting the following error
"Checking credentials: rejected. Reason: Initialization of destination ... failed. ".

Our suspicion is that this may be related to some blocked IP adresses. We saw that the following IP adress is sending packets "46.105.70.90" on ports ports 20200 and 20500. I was unable to find this IP adress in any Signavio documentation, or any SAP documentation for that matter.

I wanted to ask if anyone has encountered this problem and this IP adress before and whether anyone knows if this is an SAP related IP adress?
I would also be very happy about any solution to this issue

Hello,

Thank you for your response and for pointing me to a similar thread with the same issue.

I will check that post and follow up there.

Thanks again!

entry.title: Re: eDocument Italy - Badi EDOC_IT_ATTACHMENT XML Add attachement
entry.content: Hi I used the CL_BLE_HTTP_CLIENT and RESPONSE classes to call the GetPDF service. So I have the response (in XML format application/json in the header is ignored). How do I get the BillingDocumentBinary tag? Are there public classes to decode Json or XML tags from responses string?

entry.title: Local ATC Checks fail with incorrect message "Execution of check seemingly aborted"
entry.content:
Hello,

When running ATC checks with any variant, the result is a failure with warning message "Execution of check seemingly aborted" - which is incorrect.
We are running the ATC Check locally on our S/4 Development System (no rfc involved). We have already implemented SAP Notes 3232321 and 3163848.
All guides I've found so far seem to be for remote checks from a central ATC System, which does not apply to us, so I'd be happy about any tips.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure "standard text key" for task list &...
entry.content:
If you are unable to find the "Define Standard Text Key" option in SPRO,
follow these steps to configure and use the Standard Text Key correctly in SAP PM:

Step 1: Check Configuration for Standard Text Key
1.Go to SPRO (SAP Customizing Implementation Guide)
- Navigate to:
Plant Maintenance and Customer Service → Maintenance Plans, Work Centers, Task Lists → Task Lists → General Data
- Look for an option related toStandard Text Key.
- If you cannot find it, check if the relevant configuration is available under:
Plant Maintenance and Customer Service → Maintenance and Service Processing → Basic Settings → Define Standard Text Key

2. If the option is still not visible, ensure that you have the necessary authorization to make changes in SPRO.

Step 2: Assign Standard Text Key to Task List
1. UseTransaction IA01 (Create Equipment Task List).
2. In theOperation Details, navigate to theExternal Processing tab.
3. Enter theStandard Text Key in the relevant field.

Step 3: Use Standard Text Key in Maintenance Order
1. UseTransaction IW31 (Create Maintenance Order).
2. When selecting aTask List, the Standard Text Key should automatically be copied into the order operation.

Step 4: Maintain Standard Texts in SO10
1. UseTransaction SO10 to create predefined texts.
2. Link the Standard Texts to the Standard Text Key.

Alternative Solution (If Standard Text Key Is Not Available)
If the "Define Standard Text Key" option is not available in SPRO, you can achieve similar functionality using:
-Work Instructions in Task Lists
-Long Text in Operations (IA01, IA02, IA03)
-Catalog Profiles (Custom Codes for Standard Texts)

entry.title: Re: Datasphere replication flow Initial load in progress...
entry.content:
We identified the root cause why data is not loading for I_XXXXXXX . This is due to Datasphere Model editor bug which introduced wrong datatype definition for columns IntcoExtActlTransfOfCtrlDteTme, IntcoIntActlTransfOfCtrlDteTme, IntcoExtPlndTransfOfCtrlDteTme, and IntcoExtActlTransfOfCtrlDteTme timestamp columns.

This bug has been fixed in Datasphere version 2025.3.66.

Option 1: Go to Data Builder Replication Flow editor, delete I_XXXXXXX object., add the object. Then deploy. If you do not want to delete the replication object, then follow these manual steps 2-9

2. Go to Data Builder editor

3. Use Edit -> “Export to CSN/JSON File” from the replication flow editor and export the CSN definition of the RF

4. Open the exported file, search for $DYNAMIC.decimal_undefined_undefined under the section “replicationTasks” and replace it with com.sap.core.timestamp. Repeat this for all occurrences of $DYNAMIC.decimal_undefined_undefined in the file and replace with com.sap.core.timestamp

5. After the above changes are made, save the file.

6. Navigate to Data Builder landing page and import the above file using the option Import Objects from CSN/JSON File

7. Deploy the replication flow from the landing page (Please do not deploy the replication flow from the editor)

Please note that when you modify the replication flow definition externally, you should only change the recommended fields as any wrong changes can break the replication flow.

entry.title: Re: Manage your project documentation using SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
hello, 

thanks for description of functionalities for DOCUMENTATION IN ALM. Just a question, pls, is there a possibility to use of 1 common document(e.g. Handbook) across different projects in ALM? 

We´d like to share description of some "common steps" between more different projects. We hope that this could help us keep the information in the most up-to-date form and we will not have to go back every time.
I will explain more specifically: for example, we create a business partner the same way in different projects. We would like to describe this process and save it as 1 common documentation, which we would like to pin to each project in which the BP will be created. If something changes in the process, and we would add this change to the"global" documentation, we would be sure that the information will reach all projects and will be the same.

Is this possible to do it in ALM? thanks in advance for your help Olga

entry.title: How can workflow approvers edit fields such as cost-center and WBS elements when approving workflow?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am wondering how an approver in a journal entry verification workflow can change or edit some fields in the transaction such as cost elements or tax codes without having to reject it and make a comment?

I have not been able to find any documentation. Please let me know if you have any suggestions or answers.

entry.title: Inquiry About Recovering Deleted Root Parent
entry.content:
Dear SEN Support Team,

We are reaching out to request assistance in recovering the Root Parent, which was accidentally deleted. As a result, all content that was in our local Producer now appears as "Unclassified" in the Manager.

We attempted to resolve the issue by creating a new Root, but the material is still not displaying correctly. Could you please guide us on the proper procedure to restore the original Root and reorganize the content in the Manager?

We look forward to your response and appreciate your help in advance.

Best regards,

Hi

  How to check the reason behind failure of Email message in Sent Items.

When in User Setup email id of user is entered then email does not go. When SBO Mailer email id is entered then email works

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How do I make the Security Policy field a mandatory field in transaction SU01?
entry.content: Asking and Answering Questions in the Community After Migration or forum help qa

entry.title: Code d'invitation Bitrue : TAWVLWV
entry.content:
Qu'est-ce que le code d'invitation Bitrue ?

Offrant une opportunité spéciale aux nouveaux utilisateurs de la plateforme Bitrue, le code d'invitation "TAWVLWV" offre une réduction de commission de 25 %. Grâce à ce code, les nouveaux utilisateurs bénéficient d'une réduction importante sur les frais de transaction et peuvent également bénéficier de bonus et d'autres avantages. Ce code, qui offre un grand avantage à ceux qui souhaitent trader des crypto-monnaies, leur permet de trader avec des frais de transaction moins élevés pendant le trading.

Le code TAWVLWV est valable à vie sur la plateforme Bitrue et offre la réduction de commission la plus élevée et l'accès à d'autres avantages sur la plateforme. Ce code crée une excellente opportunité pour ceux qui veulent se lancer dans le trading de crypto-monnaies.

entry.title: Re: SAC Advance Formula Error while copying data from one...
entry.content: With Cross model copy, I can only map one property per dimension. There is no possibility of mapping multiple properties of single dimension. Also I need advance formula because I have some additional logic based on version to copy number of periods.

entry.title: Compliance Performance Visibility: Exploring the New Compliance Overview Dashboard
entry.content:
Managing environmental compliance can often feel overwhelming—tracking overdue tasks, notices of violation, deviations, and incidents all at once is no small task. 

But what if you could have ongoing visibility into these aspects? Enter the new Compliance Overview dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud—a game-changer for your EHS team looking to get better transparency into compliance performance and focus on what matters most. 

 

What’s on the Dashboard?

Key Metrics at a Glance 

  • Tasks: A breakdown of the compliance tasks by priority and status. 
  • Deviations: Number of recorded compliance deviations. 
  • Notices of Violation: Count of issued notices, paired with total penalty amounts. 
  • Environmental Incidents: A snapshot of incidents categorized by type (e.g. air, water, etc.). 
  • Overdue Tasks: Total count of compliance tasks that have surpassed their due dates. 

By adjusting the Plant and Priority filters at the top, you can tailor all these metrics to focus on specific locations or priorities, giving you targeted insights for precise decision-making. 

 

Zooming in: Interactive Graphs 

Tasks Overview 

Compliance tasks are reactive or proactive action steps, which help you minimize your environmental impact or prevent environmental incidents and deviations. 

The Tasks Overview graph displays these tasks filtered by priority (e.g. Very High, Medium, Low) and status (e.g. Scheduled, Overdue, Completed), providing a clear view of task progress year-to-date (YTD). While this is the default setup, the dashboard’s flexibility allows you to adjust the graph types and display information based on your preference. 

 

Deviations  

A deviation refers to a departure from approved procedures, standards, permits or regulations, i.e. it’s used to handle cases of non-conformance to specific environmental requirements. 

The Deviations graph provides a YTD breakdown similar to the Tasks Overview, categorizing the deviations by origin (e.g. Permit, Policy, etc.) and type (Reportable or Nonreportable) offering customizable visualizations that help track patterns and adjust focus areas. With this flexibility, you can tailor the data display to identify trends and prioritize corrective actions. 

 

Notices of Violation 

A notice of violation is a formal warning from regulatory authorities indicating a breach of an environmental regulation. 

In this graph, you’ll see both the total penalty amounts and the number of violations recorded. Using the dropdown field, you can adjust what’s displayed on the graph, offering a dynamic view of the data that’s most relevant to your compliance goals. 

 

Environmental Incidents 

An environmental incident is any unplanned event that harms the environment, such as hazardous spills, pollutant emissions, or waste discharges affecting air, water, soil, etc. 

The Environmental Incidents section categorizes these events by release type, such as air, ground, water, or your custom release. You can use filters to view specific types or display all categories for a broader view. This flexibility helps pinpoint areas requiring immediate attention or analyze trends across multiple types of incidents. 

Tracking Trends 

The Compliance Overview dashboard also provides insights to trend analysis: 

 

Incidents Over Time 

This trend graph displays the number of incidents by type (e.g., deviations, violations, etc.) over time, allowing you to identify recurring patterns, pinpoint high-risk periods, and address them proactively. 

 

Overdue Tasks Trend 

This graph shows the monthly trend of overdue tasks for the past six months of the current calendar year. It helps you spot bottlenecks and assess your team's capacity to handle compliance tasks within deadlines. 

Why All This Matters 

The Compliance Overview dashboard isn’t just a collection of numbers—it’s a tool designed to empower environmental managers: 

  • Identify Risks: By visualizing overdue tasks, deviations, and incidents, you can quickly pinpoint areas of concern and take corrective action. 
  • Save Time: With all critical metrics in one place, you spend less time hunting for data and more time solving problems. 
  • Always Up-To-Date: The dashboard pulls live data directly from EHS applications, ensuring the insights you’re working with are accurate. 
  • Adaptable to Your Needs: The graphs and filters are fully customizable, allowing you to tailor the dashboard to fit your team’s unique priorities and workflows. 

With the Compliance Overview dashboard, you gain the clarity and control needed to turn complex data into actionable insights. Whether you’re tracking trends, managing tasks, or assessing deviations, this tool ensures you’re always one step ahead.

entry.title: Mã mời Bitrue: TAWVLWV
entry.content:
Mã mời Bitrue là gì?

Mã mời "TAWVLWV" cung cấp cơ hội đặc biệt cho người dùng mới tham gia nền tảng Bitrue, cung cấp mức chiết khấu hoa hồng 25%. Nhờ mã này, người dùng mới được giảm đáng kể phí giao dịch và cũng có thể hưởng lợi từ tiền thưởng và các lợi thế khác. Mã này, mang đến lợi thế lớn cho những người muốn giao dịch tiền điện tử, cho phép họ giao dịch với mức phí giao dịch thấp hơn trong quá trình giao dịch.

Mã TAWVLWV có hiệu lực trọn đời trên nền tảng Bitrue và cung cấp mức chiết khấu hoa hồng cao nhất cùng quyền truy cập vào các lợi thế khác trên nền tảng. Mã này tạo ra cơ hội tuyệt vời cho những người muốn bắt đầu giao dịch tiền điện tử.

entry.title: Content-type when calling web hook from Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to call a an API on Service Now as a WebHook from SAP Event Mesh . The API only accepts the content-type application/json, but Event Mesh sets the content type to application/cloudevent+json, despite our settings.

We have configured the web hook to have a default content type of application/json, and I can see in the cloud event coming from S/4 that the content type is set as application/json

See attached screenshots of config and the relevant envelope.

What can we do make sure we always use application/json when calling this web hook?

Regards

Mattias

entry.title: Re: End of Maintenance for Basic Authentication
entry.content: The SAP Community and SAP Help Portal are accessible by customers and partners. The "Deprecation Details” and “Additional Resources” entries give details of which features are covered by the article.

entry.title: Re: POINT 7 & 11 SALES ORDER
entry.content:
Hello,

Open the sales order and click in this icon

Then make sure that in the "Quantity" column you have checked to be "Active".

Best regards

entry.title: Código de invitación de Bitrue: TAWVLWV
entry.content:
¿Qué es el código de invitación de Bitrue?

El código de invitación "TAWVLWV" ofrece una oportunidad especial para los nuevos usuarios de la plataforma Bitrue y les ofrece un descuento del 25 % en la comisión. Gracias a este código, los nuevos usuarios obtienen un descuento significativo en las tarifas de transacción y también pueden beneficiarse de bonificaciones y otras ventajas. Este código, que ofrece una gran ventaja para quienes desean operar con criptomonedas, les permite operar con tarifas de transacción más bajas durante la negociación.

El código TAWVLWV es válido de por vida en la plataforma Bitrue y ofrece el mayor descuento en la comisión y acceso a otras ventajas en la plataforma. Este código crea una gran oportunidad para quienes desean comenzar a operar con criptomonedas.

entry.title: ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud: configuration, performance, sizing
entry.content:
ABAP Data Integration for SAP Business Data Cloud introduces a new technology called ABAP Push Replication Service (APRS). APRS pushes the data of CDS views, which are part of Data Products, into HANA Data Lake Files (HDLFS).

In this blog I want to give you an overview of the APRS and introduce you to important performance settings.

Connection Configuration

The rather complex task of connections configuration between SAP BDC, APRS and HDLFS is described in the SAP Note 3539174 - Integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with SAP Business Data Cloud. We assume it has been done and everything is working.

 

Introduction to APRS

The operation of APRS is based on several background tasks (the way how jobs are working you can see on  Figure 2):

- (ACD) Job and Access Plan Calculation (ACP) Job: processes for calculating the optimal transfer of an initial snapshot of the CDS View. Those processes capture the initial snapshot of object and start data transfer upon completing the calculation of portion. The process is especially important for transferring large objects, as it determines the parameters for parallelizing data transfer.

On Figure 1 you can find an example how CDS view is divided into ACD partitions, access plans and portions. ACD partitions are necessary to parallelize the execution of pre-calculation of CDS views where physical table partitioning exists.

A single access plan contains a 10 Gb of data and is further divided into 100 portions, 100 Mb each. Access Plan Pre-Calculation Job creates access plans. Access plan is restricted by the boundaries, which are conditions on the key fields of a CDS view. Portion has no boundaries, but simple a block of data 100 Mn size.

Parallelization of ACD jobs for a CDS view is only  possible when main table contain DB partitions. Parallelization of ACP jobs depend on the number of access plans for the CDS view.

Figure 1: Access Plan calculation concept

- Observer Job: monitors changes in logging tables, put new request in the queue for data transfer, starts the transfer jobs if they are not already running.

- Transfer Job: the process responsible for data transfer. It handles both the initial data load and the transfer of delta.

- Health Check Job: performs various functions related to monitoring the service's health (like starting missing observer jobs, verifying the status of portions that have been in process for a long time or verifying the daemon status), and as part of it health check calculates the optimal number of required transfer jobs. By default, it runs every 10 minutes.

Since the APRS relies on background processes, it is essential to ensure the correct configuration of the technical job framework before using the service. The list of required background jobs and links to configuration instructions can be found in SAP Note 3500131 - ABAP Data Integration for Business Data Cloud.

Figure 2: Job diagram

Access plan Calculation job and Transfer job are running during initial load, Observer job and Transfer job are running during delta replication. Health Check job is always running when at least one subscription exists.

APRS contains a number of parameters for fine-tuning system performance (see Figure 3). By default, all parameters are set , obtained from testing of standard use cases. Some parameters require adjustments if it is necessary to allocate more resources to APRS than were defined by the default settings. The settings that affect performance can be modified using the DHADM tool and are shown in Figure 3.

Figure 3: Job Settings

Note: by default parameter names are displayed in a readable format. You can switch to the technical parameter names through the menu: Utilities -> Display Parameter Name

 

Configuring Initial Load

Access Plan Calculation

With the implemented SAP TCI Notes ABAP Integration - released October 2024 (see SAP Note 3500131 - ABAP Data Integration for Business Data Cloud) is used to orchestrate ACD and ACP jobs.

Note: The parameter "/1DH/ACS*" / "Maximum Number of Jobs for ABAP Daemon" (ACP_MAX_JOBS) defines the maximum number of background jobs that can be allocated for ACP processes. The default value (-1) means that the number of background jobs is unlimited, but it cannot exceed 80% of all configured background processes in the system.

The access plan pre-calculation job determines the optimal conditions for the following parallelization of access plan calculation. The parameters are calculated according to the amount of data required for transfer. The parameters are described in SAP Note 3559452 - ABAP Data Integration - Automatic parallelization for access plan calculation jobs.

Figure 4: Jobs for access plan calculation

The DHADM transaction allows you to change the default values for the object priority and the maximum number of background processes for each object individually.

Transfer Jobs

Transfer Job performs the transferring data to the target system. The same Job can transfer data of initial load and delta load. All parameters for the Transfer job are relevant for both initial loading and delta replication. Here we will consider all the parameters affecting the transfer job.

The initial data transfer to HDLFS consists of several phases: reading of data from intermediate tables, converting data to CSV format and writing data to the data lake. Therefore, the speed of data transfer in HDLFS depends on numerous APRS parameters and the performance of the system itself.

During the initial load, the unit of data transfer is a portion created during the ACP calculation. This means that multiple portions can be transferred through several parallel processes. See Figure 4 where you can find tab Portions. On this tab you find list of all calculated portions that are ready for transfer process. Column “Portion Status” tells what is actual transfer status for portion: Open – transfer not yet started, In Process, Finished or Failed – if any error occurs during transfer. The "Transfer End Time" column is filled in if the transfer of this portion is completed without errors (see Figure 5).

Figure 5: List of Portions for Data Transfer

By default, the number of Transfer Jobs is set to 1. If the number of objects is very large or the amount of data in one or several objects is large (for example, the CDS View I_GLAccountLineItemRawData is traditionally the largest object and often contains billions of records), it highly recommended increase the number of parallel jobs (parameter TRANSFER_MAX_JOBS). The parameters "/1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA" / "Minimum Number of Data Transfer Jobs" (TRANSFER_MIN_JOBS) and "Maximum Number of Data Transfer Jobs" (TRANSFER_MAX_JOBS) define the lower and upper limits for the number of concurrently running background processes. The Health Check calculates the required number of processes and schedules new ones in the configured range (TRANSFER_MIN_JOBS – TRANSFER_MAX_JOBS) if required with each run. The number of running processes can be seen in DHADM in the Transfer Overview section under the Transfer Jobs tab (see Figure 6).

Figure 6: List of active Transfer Jobs

The parameter "/1DH/PUSH_CDS_DELTA" / "Minimum Runtime for Data Transfer Job" (TRANSFER_MIN_RUNTIME_SEC) is necessary to set the minimum "lifetime" of the background process. This is necessary in cases where there are a large number of changes in the system with a short time between changes. If the job does not find data to work with, it immediately stops, and starting a new job takes a certain amount of time. And additionally, to avoid generating a large number of jobs with a short lifetime, this parameter can be used to set the time the process will wait for new data to be available for transferring. To avoid degrading system performance, it is not recommended to set the parameter below the default value.

 

Configurations for Delta Replication

Observer Jobs

If the CDS View is subscribed for delta replication, a set of Master and Subscriber Logging tables is created for each table described in the corresponding annotations to the CDS View. Any changes made to the data in the table are recorded in the corresponding Master Logging table. The Observer Job monitors the contents of the Master Logging tables and copies these records to the Subscriber Logging tables with simultaneous transformations necessary to increase data transfer speed. At the same time, a new entry is created in the queue for the Transfer Job. By default, only one Observer Job is running in the system, which is sufficient in most cases for copying changes in the system. If there are significant changes in the system, with several million records per second in different tables, and you observe that the number of records in the "No. of Records in Master Logging Table" column in the Recording Overview section of DHADM is not decreasing, you can increase the number of Observer Jobs by changing the parameters "/1DH/OBSERVE_LOGTAB" / "Maximum Number of Observer Jobs" (OBSERVER_MAX_JOBS) or "Minimum Number of Observer Jobs" (OBSERVER_MIN_JOBS). After the next run of the Health Check Job, the number of Observer Jobs will change within the established values. If the minimum and maximum amounts are different, the number of background processes will change automatically depending on the system's needs.

By default, the lifetime of a single Observer Job is set to one hour. If necessary, for example, if the number of changes in the system is small, you can change the parameters "/1DH/OBSERVE_LOGTAB" / "Observer Job Runtime" (OBSERVER_MIN_RUNTIME) to adjust the runtime of the Observer Job and "Observer Job Wait Time" (OBSERVER_PERIOD), which determines the pause between the next run of the Observer Job. Keep in mind that if you change the parameter "Observer Job Wait Time", you will increase latency the processing of changes.

Figure 7: List of active Observer Jobs

Transfer Jobs

As mentioned earlier, the transfer job works both during the initial data load and delta replication. You can find information about the parameters directly affecting the transfer job in the description for initial load phase. Here you will find additional information about the transfer process that is relevant only during delta replication.

During delta load, changes are accumulated in special buffer tables (RDB) and transferred as enough data is collected or after some time interval has passed. By default the transfer process starts if one of two conditions is met: either the size of the changes exceed 10 Mb or the changes accumulate in buffer over 10 minutes.

Note: the Delta Data Transfer Daemon (see Figure 2) is observe the conditions for delta buffer and creates a new queue entry of conditions are fulfil.

During the subscription start, the system automatically calculates two parameters for the buffer table: the maximum buffer size (in number of records) and the size of one packet (also measured in the number of records).

The buffer is regularly replenished by reconstructing the modified data. When the buffer reaches the maximum number of records, it is blocked for writing and waits for the moment when the packets formed from the buffer data will be transferred to the target. The packets are formed by the transfer job itself before the data transfer starts. And package is the smallest unit for the transfer process during delta replication.

Therefore, the buffer size and packet size are important for optimal data transfer speed. Ideally, we need to achieve parameters such that the buffer never overflows, and the packets are of sufficient size for the transfer process to handle them quickly enough.

Note: the complete list of all job settings parameters is described in SAP Note 3223735.

 

Performance / Sizing

Based on the service's experience with large volumes of replicable data, here are some recommendations for fine-tuning the previously mentioned parameters.

Access Plan Calculation

After installing SAP Note 3559452, adjusting parameters to increase ACP performance is not required. It should be mentioned that the speed of the pre-calculation process depends on the presence of partitions for the main table in the CDS View. In this case, even the pre-calculation process is carried out in several parallel processes. Partitioning is especially recommended for the ACDOCA table, which is the basis table for the CDS View I_GLAccountLineItemRawData, as well as for other tables containing huge volumes of data.

Observer Jobs

The Observer Job does not affect the initial load and is only relevant for delta replication. You should only increase the number of jobs if you observe a significant number of records in the Master Logging Tables and this number either does not decrease significantly over a long period of time or even grows. Do not enable the collection of Observer Job statistics unless it is necessary to analyze the process's throughput. Do not leave the statistics collection enabled for more than 1-2 days.

Transfer Jobs

When calculating the required resources for data transfer, keep in mind that all objects have different record lengths. With the same number of records, an object with 2-5 fields will load several times faster than an object where one record consists of 400 fields. But you should also not forget about the field types. The same small table can consist entirely of string fields where each value can contain megabytes of data.

Throughput measurements on large data volumes show an approximate size of 5-6 megabytes per second.

If you have objects like the CDS View I_GLAccountLineItemRawData containing billions of records, it makes sense to configure 40-50 background processes in the system and set the maximum number of processes for transfer jobs to 30-35. The minimum number of processes in this case is recommended to be 10. In this case, APRS has the ability to flexibly adjust the number of transfer jobs, and the data transfer for a single object will be maximally parallelized.

At the end of the initial load, the number of background processes can be significantly reduced. Depending on the volume of the changes in the system, it is recommended to set the number of running background processes to between 1 and 10.

 

Configuration example

Measurements on some test systems show the following metrics for the "large" object I_GLAccountLineItemRawData (consists of 437 fields):
Calculation time for one single standard access plan takes from 300 to 420 sec. One access plan consists of 100 portions. One portion contains ~16000 records.
Transfer time for one portion is from 15 to 23 sec.

We suggest follow formula to estimate number of batch processes necessary to fulfil calculation phase in desirable time frame:

N(max number of ACP jobs) = S(number of records in  CDS View) / T(sec in time frame) * F(number of fields in CDS View) / 350.000

Example: Assuming a view having 437 fields and 3 billion records you want to finish calculation phase in 24 hours. 3.000.000.000 / 86400 (sec in one day) * 437 / 350.000 (average number of processed field in one second) = 45 access plan jobs will be required.

 

CSV generation is a CPU-intensive task. Transfer performance can be roughly estimated at 150,000 - 300,000 cells/sec. This varies greatly depending on the actual field content and data types.

We suggest follow formula to estimate number of batch processes necessary to fulfil data transfer phase in desirable time frame:

N(max number of transfer jobs) = S(number of records in  CDS View) / T(sec in time frame) * F(number of fields in CDS View) / 300.000

Example: Assuming a view having 437 fields and 3 billion records you want to load the data in 24 hours. 3.000.000.000 / 86400 (sec in one day) * 437 / 300.000 (number of processing field in one second) = 50 transfer jobs will be required.

 

Taking in account that transfer process starts as soon as first access plan is calculated and further both phases running parallel, our tests have shown following numbers for CDS view I_GLAccountLineItemRawData containing 3 billions records:

  • pre-calculation and calculation in 10 parallel jobs takes ~1.5 days
  • transfer time in 30 parallel jobs takes ~1.5-2 days

entry.title: Re: Designing Responsive Layout in the New Optimized Story Experience
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

I have a question regarding widget communication in SAP applications. I have a panel that is closed by default, and when the user opens it, I want the adjacent table to resize accordingly. However, I've encountered an issue: the setWidth function only accepts pixel values, not grid values.

Is there a recommended approach to make two widgets communicate in this way? How can I effectively resize the table based on the panel's state? Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Kod zaproszeniowy Bitrue: TAWVLWV
entry.content:
Co to jest kod zaproszeniowy Bitrue?

Oferując specjalną okazję dla nowych użytkowników platformy Bitrue, kod zaproszeniowy „TAWVLWV” zapewnia 25% zniżki na prowizję. Dzięki tem kodowi nowi użytkownicy otrzymują znaczną zniżkę na opłaty transakcyjne, a także mogą skorzystać z bonusów i innych korzyści. Ten kod, który zapewnia dużą zaletę dla tych, którzy chcą handlować kryptowalutami, pozwala im handlować z niższymi opłatami transakcyjnymi podczas handlu.

Kod TAWVLWV jest ważny dożywotnio na platformie Bitrue i oferuje najwyższą zniżkę na prowizję oraz dostęp do innych korzyści na platformie. Ten kod stwarza świetną okazję dla tych, którzy chcą rozpocząć handel kryptowalutami.
